artefakt.foundation

The record
is the argument.

Founded by Jon Lowenstein Guggenheim Fellow National Geographic Explorer TED Senior Fellow Founding Member, NOOR Images Calgary, Treaty 7 Territory Chicago, South Side
Ukraine, 2022
01 — Witness

Long-form work.
Sustained attention.

Artefakt exists to produce and support documentary work built on time — months and years in place, not days. Work where the photographer stayed long enough for the place to get into the photographs.

From Chicago's South Side to Ukraine, from North Korea to the communities forming around our Calgary gallery.

View the work
Workshop, Zurich 2018
02 — Connect

Fellowship.
Academy. Platform.

The Artefakt Fellowship supports emerging documentary practitioners in Calgary and Chicago. The Academy brings visiting practitioners into direct conversation with the next generation through the Bearing Witness lecture series.

Fellowship and Academy
North Korea
03 — Legacy

Archive.
Education. Memory.

A permanent, publicly accessible archive of documentary work — photographs, film, and collected materials — treated as infrastructure rather than inventory. The archive is not nostalgia. It is an operating system for what comes next.

Explore the archive
South Side, Chicago
04 — Sustain

Nothing of value
gets thrown away.

Artefakt runs an ethical materials reuse and e-cycling operation. Discarded electronics, recovered equipment, and surplus materials are repaired, resold, or redistributed — generating revenue that funds the other three pillars.

The Sustain program
TED Talk — Jon Lowenstein
05 — About

Built from
inside the work.

Artefakt Foundation is a documentary photography, film, and arts institution operating across Canada and the United States. Founded by Jon Lowenstein, whose practice spans more than twenty years of sustained documentary work.

An Alberta Society. Independent. Supported by people who believe the photograph is evidence.

About Artefakt
